G, H, I and J sit in row 1 while M, N, O, P, Q and R
sit in row 2. Students in row 1 and row 2 face
each other. Students of row 1 face south while
students of row 2 face north.

One person sits between O and Q, who sits at
one of the extreme ends. F does not sit at any
extreme end. M sits to the immediate right of N,
who sits fifth left to the Q. R sits opposite I, who
is not adjacent to F. I sits immediately right to
the H. J sits second to the right of E but not
opposite to P.
How many students sit between M and Q in the
same row?

question:
Study the following data carefully and answer
the questions accordingly.
Ten people sit in a two-parallel row. Five people
in each row. In row-1 A, B, C, D and E are seated
and all face towards south. In row-2 P, Q, R, S
and T are seated and all face towards the
north. People seated in row-1 face people
seated in row-2. S sits in the middle of the row.
The immediate neighbor of S faces E. E sits
three places away from B. T sits diagonally
opposite to B. A faces P. C and Q does not sit
on any of the extreme ends of the row. D sits
right of B.
Four of the following five are alike in a certain
way based on their positions in the above
arrangement and so form a group. Which of the
following does not belong to the group ?

between I and J.

who faces Q.
Here, vacant seat lie between K and J in both
the cases.

Here, Vacant seat lie between Q and S in case
1.

faces the one who sits to the immediate left of

Here, case 2 gets eliminated.
12) M sits second to the left of L.
R does not belong to that group.

who faces A?
questionHtml:

answer the questions accordingly.

persons on one row faces towards the person of
the other row and vice versa. Each person likes
different colours viz. Black, Blue, Cyan, Gold,
Green, Grey, Indigo, Pink, Red and Rose. All the
information is not necessarily in the same order.
E sits at one of the extreme ends and third to
the left of the one who likes Grey. Only one
person sits between the one who likes Grey and
I, who faces the one who likes Blue.G sits
adjacent to
the one who likes Blue and
opposite to the one who likes Gold.The one who
likes Rose is the immediate neighbour of the
one who likes Gold and third to the left of A.The
one who likes Green sits adjacent to the one
who likes Cyan and the one who faces A.H sits
facing towards the one who likes Cyan and
immediate left of C who faces B.J sits to the
immediate right of D and faces towards the
one who likes Black. F faces towards the one
who likes Indigo but neither faces north nor likes
Red.
How many persons sit between D and the one
who faces A?
explanation:
1. E sits at one of the extreme ends and third to
the left of the one who likes Grey. Only one
person sits between the one who likes Grey and
I, who faces the one who likes Blue.
2. G sits adjacent to the one who likes Blue and
opposite to the one who likes Gold. The one
who likes Rose is the immediate neighbour of
the one who likes Gold and third to the left of A.
3. The one who likes Green sits adjacent to the
one who likes Cyan and the one who faces A. H
sits facing towards the one who likes Cyan and
immediate left of C who faces B.
4. J sits to the immediate right of D and faces
towards the one who likes Black. F faces
towards the one who likes Indigo but neither
faces north nor likes Red. Hence, cases 1, 2 and
2a get eliminated.
Final arrangement:
Answer: Two

person sits between the one who likes Grey and
I, who faces the one who likes Blue.
2. G sits adjacent to the one who likes Blue and
opposite to the one who likes Gold. The one
who likes Rose is the immediate neighbour of
the one who likes Gold and third to the left of A.
3. The one who likes Green sits adjacent to the
one who likes Cyan and the one who faces A. H
sits facing towards the one who likes Cyan and
immediate left of C who faces B.
4. J sits to the immediate right of D and faces

towards the one who likes Indigo but neither

Final arrangement:
Answer: Two
